en.extract:
The rufous-banded honeyeater (Conopophila albogularis) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found in the Aru Islands, New Guinea and northern Australia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical mangrove forests. The rufous-banded honeyeater is considered one of the most common small birds in the suburban ecosystem of Darwin, Australia, notable because its lack of introduced bird species. It is also native to Queensland, Australia. Ornithologist F. Salomonsen recognized two subspecies of rufous-banded honeyeater, Conopophila albogularis and Conopophila mimikae but J. Ford disagreed with this subspeciation.

The '''rufous-banded honeyeater''' ('''''Conopophila albogularis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is found in the [[Aru Islands]], [[New Guinea]] and northern [[Australia]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical mangrove [[forest]]s. The rufous-banded honeyeater is considered one of the most common small birds in the suburban ecosystem of [[Darwin, Northern Territory|Darwin]], Australia, notable because its lack of [[introduced species|introduced bird species]].<ref name="noske1998">{{cite journal | last = Noske | first = R.A. | title = Breeding biology, demography and success of the rufous-banded honeyeater, Conopophila albogularis, in Darwin, a monsoonal tropical city | journal = Wildlife Research | volume = 25 | issue = 4 | pages = 339–356 | doi = 10.1071/WR97070 | date = 1998 | url = https://www.publish.csiro.au/wr/WR97070| url-access = subscription }}</ref> It is also native to Queensland, Australia.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Species profile—Conopophila albogularis (rufous-banded honeyeater) |url=https://apps.des.qld.gov.au/species-search/details/?id=1536 |access-date=2024-04-04 |website=Species information |publisher=Queensland Government}}</ref> Ornithologist [[Finn Salomonsen|F. Salomonsen]] recognized two subspecies of rufous-banded honeyeater, ''Conopophila albogularis'' and ''Conopophila mimikae'' but [[Julian Ralph Ford|J. Ford]] disagreed with this subspeciation.<ref name="ford1983">{{cite journal | last = Ford | first = J. | title = Taxonomic notes on some mangrove-inhabiting birds in Australasia | journal = Records of the Western Australian Museum | volume = 10 | date = 1983 | pages = 381–415 | issue = 4 | url = https://museum.wa.gov.au/sites/default/files/TAXONOMIC%20NOTES%20ON%20SOME%20MANGROVE-INHABITING%20BIRDS%20IN%20AUSTRALASIA.pdf}}</ref>
